repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy_v1 pursued the proxy endpoint problem but lacked this active-class latent-response completion.

published_prior_art.cite: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 supplies the general sharp discrete-SCM polynomial-program representation for declared finite latent-variable restrictions and counterfactual targets.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry gives endpoint-face exclusion for this active three-class factorization-constrained mediation set.

published_prior_art.cite: BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 supplies the no-proxy natural-effect interval; endpoint-face exclusion is a useful feasibility reformulation but not itself a demonstrated new contraction regime.

repo_prior_art.cite: No active entry states this exact response-type reduction, but it is baseline machinery rather than a new kernel.

published_prior_art.cite: Sjolander2009 derives natural-direct-effect bounds with a confounded intermediate, and BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 gives the current no-proxy mediation-bound comparator.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy_v1 already sought observable proxy endpoint exclusion; the three-active-class latent-realizable formulation is a material repair.

published_prior_art.cite: No verified source gives necessary-and-sufficient observable endpoint-exclusion inequalities plus a strictly positive rational witness for this observed-mediator, two-proxy, rank-two-for-three-active-class factorization model; GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 and ZhangSu2024 address different incomplete-proxy settings.

soundness_flags.label: Lemma 2 (endpoint lift)
soundness_flags.one_line: State the lemma conditional on \(\mathcal F_K^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)\ne\varnothing\): otherwise \(L_K^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)\), \(U_K^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)\), and the endpoint-face equivalences are undefined.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Finite active-class proxy-factorization model
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The fixed \(K\), lower active-class mass \(\kappa\), conditional-independent proxy measurements, and minor-separation \(\eta\) are accepted as a conditional sensitivity regime.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any strict endpoint certificate is claimed only under the stated finite active-class proxy-factorization model, not for unrestricted latent cardinality or asymptotic inference.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add \(\mathcal F_K^{\mathrm{fail}}(P)\ne\varnothing\) explicitly to Lemma 2 and define its endpoint claims only on that compatibility domain.

Use the quantifier-elimination handle to produce a fully specified rational positive-cell table, factorization, and endpoint-face infeasibility certificate before seeking a compact signed-minor characterization.

After obtaining a witness, state a generic open neighborhood of observed tables on which the same endpoint is excluded, separating a robust factorization-based contraction regime from a one-table computation.

recommended_next_step: Repair Lemma 2's compatibility domain, then pursue the rational witness and robust observable certificate as the field-level kernel.
